title: Model Card Refresh Loop
summary: Keeps an AI model or prompt system card current with capabilities, limits, evals, and risks.
steps: Inventory the current system version, intended users, allowed uses, disallowed uses, dependencies, and deployment context.
Attach the latest evaluation results, known failure modes, safety controls, and monitoring plan.
Compare the card against actual implementation and remove stale claims.
Update risk, privacy, fairness, and security notes based on incidents or new use cases.
Route unresolved high-impact risks to the accountable owner before launch.
stopConditions: model card is current and approved
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Model Card Refresh Loop for [system, eval_results, deployment_context].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Inventory the current system version, intended users, allowed uses, disallowed uses, dependencies, and deployment context.
- Attach the latest evaluation results, known failure modes, safety controls, and monitoring plan.
- Compare the card against actual implementation and remove stale claims.
- Update risk, privacy, fairness, and security notes based on incidents or new use cases.
- Route unresolved high-impact risks to the accountable owner before launch.

Quality gates before completion:
- version matches deployment
- evals linked
- limitations explicit
- risks have owners
- stale claims removed

Stop when one of these is true:
- model card is current and approved
- evals are missing
- risk owner blocks launch

Finish with:
- updated card
- eval links
- risk table
- approval note

Escalate for human approval before: production launch, expanding allowed use, accepting high-impact risk.
